revision.text: #redirect[[en:Kenmotsu]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

'''監物'''(げんもつ)は[[律令制]]において[[中務省]]に属した品官(ほんかん)。
== 職掌 == 監物は中務省直属の官で諸官庁の倉庫の鍵を管理・出納事務の監察に携わった。そのため鍵を実際に取り扱う中務省の[[典鑰]]や[[大蔵省]]・[[内蔵寮]]の主鑰を実質的に統率した。もともとは天皇直属の「下物職」であったらしく律令施行によって中務省に組み込まれた。下物職の名称は監物の別名として使われることがある。大・中・少の三員あったが中監物は廃止され替わりに'''監物主典'''(げんもつさかん)が設置されて事務を補助した。
== 職員 == *大監物(従五位下) 1名 *中監物(従六位上) 4名・廃止 *少監物(正七位下) 4名
*監物主典(従七位上) 新設 *史生
== 関連項目 == *[[日本の官制]]
[[category:律令制の官制|けんもつ]] {{Japanese-history-stub|けんもつ}}

revision.comment: Created page with "{{Cite sources|date=March 2021}} '''Kenmotsu''' (監物) refers to a {{ill|Hinkan|ja|品官}} (specialist official) within the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, specifically under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Ministry of the Center|ja|中務省}}. == Duties == Kenmotsu officials were directly subordinate to the Ministry of the Center and were responsible for managing the keys and inspecting the inventory-related duties of various government offices' warehouses. Th..."
revision.text:
{{Cite sources|date=March 2021}} '''Kenmotsu''' (監物) refers to a {{ill|Hinkan|ja|品官}} (specialist official) within the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, specifically under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Ministry of the Center|ja|中務省}}.
== Duties == Kenmotsu officials were directly subordinate to the Ministry of the Center and were responsible for managing the keys and inspecting the inventory-related duties of various government offices' warehouses. They oversaw the actual handling of keys by officials such as the {{ill|Ten'yaku|ja|典鑰}} of the Ministry of the Center, as well as the {{ill|Shuyaku|ja|主鑰}} in the {{ill|Ōkura-shō|ja|大蔵省 (律令制)}} and the {{ill|Naizōryō|ja|内蔵寮}}. Originally, Kenmotsu may have been directly under the emperor as part of the "Shimotsumono-shoku" (下物職). This title was later incorporated into the Ministry of the Center under the Ritsuryō system. The term "Shimotsumono-shoku" persisted as an alternative name for Kenmotsu.
There were initially three grades of Kenmotsu: Dai Kenmotsu (大監物), Chū Kenmotsu (中監物), and Shō Kenmotsu (少監物). However, the Chū Kenmotsu position was later abolished and replaced by the newly established '''Kenmotsu Sakan''' (監物主典), who assisted with administrative duties.
== Ranks and Personnel == - **Dai Kenmotsu** (従五位下, Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade): 1 position - **Chū Kenmotsu** (従六位上, Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade): 4 positions (abolished) - **Shō Kenmotsu** (正七位下, Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade): 4 positions - **Kenmotsu Sakan** (従七位上, Junior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade): Newly established - **Shisei** (史生, Clerical officers)
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Government of Japan under the Ritsuryō system|ja|日本の官制}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Ritsuryō system of Japan]] [[Category:Government positions under the Ritsuryō system]]
[[ja:監物]]

{{Cite sources|date=March 2021}} '''Kenmotsu''' (監物) refers to a {{ill|Hinkan|ja|品官}} (specialist official) within the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, specifically under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Ministry of the Center|ja|中務省}}.
== Duties == Kenmotsu officials were directly subordinate to the Ministry of the Center and were responsible for managing the keys and inspecting the inventory-related duties of various government offices' warehouses. They oversaw the actual handling of keys by officials such as the {{ill|Ten'yaku|ja|典鑰}} of the Ministry of the Center, as well as the {{ill|Shuyaku|ja|主鑰}} in the {{ill|Ōkura-shō|ja|大蔵省 (律令制)}} and the {{ill|Naizōryō|ja|内蔵寮}}. Originally, Kenmotsu may have been directly under the emperor as part of the "Shimotsumono-shoku" (下物職). This title was later incorporated into the Ministry of the Center under the Ritsuryō system. The term "Shimotsumono-shoku" persisted as an alternative name for Kenmotsu.
There were initially three grades of Kenmotsu: Dai Kenmotsu (大監物), Chū Kenmotsu (中監物), and Shō Kenmotsu (少監物). However, the Chū Kenmotsu position was later abolished and replaced by the newly established '''Kenmotsu Sakan''' (監物主典), who assisted with administrative duties.
== Ranks and Personnel == - **Dai Kenmotsu** (従五位下, Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade): 1 position - **Chū Kenmotsu** (従六位上, Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade): 4 positions (abolished) - **Shō Kenmotsu** (正七位下, Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade): 4 positions - **Kenmotsu Sakan** (従七位上, Junior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade): Newly established - **Shisei** (史生, Clerical officers)
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Government of Japan under the Ritsuryō system|ja|日本の官制}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Ritsuryō system of Japan]] [[Category:Government positions under the Ritsuryō system]]

{{Cite sources|date=March 2021}} '''Kenmotsu''' (監物) refers to a {{ill|Hinkan|ja|品官}} (specialist official) within the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, specifically under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Ministry of the Center|ja|中務省}}.
== Duties == Kenmotsu officials were directly subordinate to the Ministry of the Center and were responsible for managing the keys and inspecting the inventory-related duties of various government offices' warehouses. They oversaw the actual handling of keys by officials such as the {{ill|Ten'yaku|ja|典鑰}} of the Ministry of the Center, as well as the {{ill|Shuyaku|ja|主鑰}} in the {{ill|Ōkura-shō|ja|大蔵省 (律令制)}} and the {{ill|Naizōryō|ja|内蔵寮}}. Originally, Kenmotsu may have been directly under the emperor as part of the "Shimotsumono-shoku" (下物職). This title was later incorporated into the Ministry of the Center under the Ritsuryō system. The term "Shimotsumono-shoku" persisted as an alternative name for Kenmotsu.
There were initially three grades of Kenmotsu: Dai Kenmotsu (大監物), Chū Kenmotsu (中監物), and Shō Kenmotsu (少監物). However, the Chū Kenmotsu position was later abolished and replaced by the newly established '''Kenmotsu Sakan''' (監物主典), who assisted with administrative duties.
== Ranks and Personnel == - '''Dai Kenmotsu''' (従五位下, Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade): 1 position - '''Chū Kenmotsu''' (従六位上, Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade): 4 positions (abolished) - '''Shō Kenmotsu''' (正七位下, Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade): 4 positions - '''Kenmotsu Sakan''' (従七位上, Junior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade): Newly established - '''Shisei''' (史生, Clerical officers)
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Government of Japan under the Ritsuryō system|ja|日本の官制}}

{{Cite sources|date=March 2021}} '''Kenmotsu''' (監物) refers to a {{ill|Hinkan|ja|品官}} (specialist official) within the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, specifically under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Ministry of the Center|ja|中務省}}.
== Duties == Kenmotsu officials were directly subordinate to the Ministry of the Center and were responsible for managing the keys and inspecting the inventory-related duties of various government offices' warehouses. They oversaw the actual handling of keys by officials such as the {{ill|Ten'yaku|ja|典鑰}} of the Ministry of the Center, as well as the {{ill|Shuyaku|ja|主鑰}} in the {{ill|Ōkura-shō|ja|大蔵省 (律令制)}} and the {{ill|Naizōryō|ja|内蔵寮}}. Originally, Kenmotsu may have been directly under the emperor as part of the "Shimotsumono-shoku" (下物職). This title was later incorporated into the Ministry of the Center under the Ritsuryō system. The term "Shimotsumono-shoku" persisted as an alternative name for Kenmotsu.
There were initially three grades of Kenmotsu: Dai Kenmotsu (大監物), Chū Kenmotsu (中監物), and Shō Kenmotsu (少監物). However, the Chū Kenmotsu position was later abolished and replaced by the newly established '''Kenmotsu Sakan''' (監物主典), who assisted with administrative duties.
== Ranks and Personnel == - '''Dai Kenmotsu''' (従五位下, Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade): 1 position - '''Chū Kenmotsu''' (従六位上, Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade): 4 positions (abolished) - '''Shō Kenmotsu''' (正七位下, Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade): 4 positions - '''Kenmotsu Sakan''' (従七位上, Junior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade): Newly established - '''Shisei''' (史生, Clerical officers)
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Government of Japan under the Ritsuryō system|ja|日本の官制}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Ritsuryō system of Japan]] [[Category:Government positions under the Ritsuryō system]]

There were initially three grades of Kenmotsu: Dai Kenmotsu (大監物), Chū Kenmotsu (中監物), and Shō Kenmotsu (少監物). However, the Chū Kenmotsu position was later abolished and replaced by the newly established '''Kenmotsu Sakan''' (監物主典), who assisted with administrative duties.
== Ranks and Personnel == - '''Dai Kenmotsu''' (従五位下, Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade): 1 position - '''Chū Kenmotsu''' (従六位上, Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade): 4 positions (abolished) - '''Shō Kenmotsu''' (正七位下, Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade): 4 positions - '''Kenmotsu Sakan''' (従七位上, Junior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade): Newly established - '''Shisei''' (史生, Clerical officers)

The term "Shimotsumono-shoku" persisted as an alternative name for Kenmotsu.
There were initially three grades of Kenmotsu: Dai Kenmotsu (大監物), Chū Kenmotsu (中監物), and Shō Kenmotsu (少監物). However, the Chū Kenmotsu position was later abolished and replaced by the newly established '''Kenmotsu Sakan''' (監物主典), who assisted with administrative duties.

{{Cite sources|date=March 2021}} '''Kenmotsu''' (監物) refers to a {{ill|Hinkan|ja|品官}} (specialist official) within the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, specifically under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Ministry of the Center|ja|中務省}}.
== Duties == Kenmotsu officials were directly subordinate to the Ministry of the Center and were responsible for managing the keys and inspecting the inventory-related duties of various government offices' warehouses. They oversaw the actual handling of keys by officials such as the {{ill|Ten'yaku|ja|典鑰}} of the Ministry of the Center, as well as the {{ill|Shuyaku|ja|主鑰}} in the {{ill|Ōkura-shō|ja|大蔵省 (律令制)}} and the {{ill|Naizōryō|ja|内蔵寮}}. Originally, Kenmotsu may have been directly under the emperor as part of the "Shimotsumono-shoku" (下物職). This title was later incorporated into the Ministry of the Center under the Ritsuryō system. The term "Shimotsumono-shoku" persisted as an alternative name for Kenmotsu.
There were initially three grades of Kenmotsu: Dai Kenmotsu (大監物), Chū Kenmotsu (中監物), and Shō Kenmotsu (少監物). However, the Chū Kenmotsu position was later abolished and replaced by the newly established '''Kenmotsu Sakan''' (監物主典), who assisted with administrative duties.
== Ranks and Personnel == - '''Dai Kenmotsu''' (従五位下, Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade): 1 position - '''Chū Kenmotsu''' (従六位上, Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade): 4 positions (abolished) - '''Shō Kenmotsu''' (正七位下, Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade): 4 positions - '''Kenmotsu Sakan''' (従七位上, Junior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade): Newly established - '''Shisei''' (史生, Clerical officers)

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] to [[Category:qq-{{subst:PAGENAME}}-qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Cite sources|date=March 2021}} '''Kenmotsu''' (監物) refers to a {{ill|Hinkan|ja|品官}} (specialist official) within the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, specifically under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Ministry of the Center|ja|中務省}}.
== Duties == Kenmotsu officials were directly subordinate to the Ministry of the Center and were responsible for managing the keys and inspecting the inventory-related duties of various government offices' warehouses. They oversaw the actual handling of keys by officials such as the {{ill|Ten'yaku|ja|典鑰}} of the Ministry of the Center, as well as the {{ill|Shuyaku|ja|主鑰}} in the {{ill|Ōkura-shō|ja|大蔵省 (律令制)}} and the {{ill|Naizōryō|ja|内蔵寮}}. Originally, Kenmotsu may have been directly under the emperor as part of the "Shimotsumono-shoku" (下物職). This title was later incorporated into the Ministry of the Center under the Ritsuryō system. The term "Shimotsumono-shoku" persisted as an alternative name for Kenmotsu.
There were initially three grades of Kenmotsu: Dai Kenmotsu (大監物), Chū Kenmotsu (中監物), and Shō Kenmotsu (少監物). However, the Chū Kenmotsu position was later abolished and replaced by the newly established '''Kenmotsu Sakan''' (監物主典), who assisted with administrative duties.
== Ranks and Personnel == - '''Dai Kenmotsu''' (従五位下, Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade): 1 position - '''Chū Kenmotsu''' (従六位上, Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade): 4 positions (abolished) - '''Shō Kenmotsu''' (正七位下, Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade): 4 positions - '''Kenmotsu Sakan''' (従七位上, Junior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade): Newly established - '''Shisei''' (史生, Clerical officers)
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Government of Japan under the Ritsuryō system|ja|日本の官制}}

== Ranks and Personnel == - '''Dai Kenmotsu''' (従五位下, Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade): 1 position - '''Chū Kenmotsu''' (従六位上, Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade): 4 positions (abolished) - '''Shō Kenmotsu''' (正七位下, Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade): 4 positions - '''Kenmotsu Sakan''' (従七位上, Junior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade): Newly established - '''Shisei''' (史生, Clerical officers)

{{Cite sources|date=March 2021}} '''Kenmotsu''' (監物) refers to a {{ill|Hinkan|ja|品官}} (specialist official) within the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, specifically under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Ministry of the Center|ja|中務省}}.
== Duties == Kenmotsu officials were directly subordinate to the Ministry of the Center and were responsible for managing the keys and inspecting the inventory-related duties of various government offices' warehouses. They oversaw the actual handling of keys by officials such as the {{ill|Ten'yaku|ja|典鑰}} of the Ministry of the Center, as well as the {{ill|Shuyaku|ja|主鑰}} in the {{ill|Ōkura-shō|ja|大蔵省 (律令制)}} and the {{ill|Naizōryō|ja|内蔵寮}}. Originally, Kenmotsu may have been directly under the emperor as part of the "Shimotsumono-shoku" (下物職). This title was later incorporated into the Ministry of the Center under the Ritsuryō system. The term "Shimotsumono-shoku" persisted as an alternative name for Kenmotsu.
There were initially three grades of Kenmotsu: Dai Kenmotsu (大監物), Chū Kenmotsu (中監物), and Shō Kenmotsu (少監物). However, the Chū Kenmotsu position was later abolished and replaced by the newly established '''Kenmotsu Sakan''' (監物主典), who assisted with administrative duties.
== Ranks and Personnel == - '''Dai Kenmotsu''' (従五位下, Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade): 1 position - '''Chū Kenmotsu''' (従六位上, Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade): 4 positions (abolished) - '''Shō Kenmotsu''' (正七位下, Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade): 4 positions - '''Kenmotsu Sakan''' (従七位上, Junior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade): Newly established - '''Shisei''' (史生, Clerical officers)
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Government of Japan under the Ritsuryō system|ja|日本の官制}}

{{Cite sources|date=March 2021}} '''Kenmotsu''' (監物) refers to a {{ill|Hinkan|ja|品官}} (specialist official) within the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, specifically under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Ministry of the Center|ja|中務省|1=Ministry of the Center}}.
== Duties == Kenmotsu officials were directly subordinate to the Ministry of the Center and were responsible for managing the keys and inspecting the inventory-related duties of various government offices' warehouses. They oversaw the actual handling of keys by officials such as the {{ill|Ten'yaku|ja|典鑰}} of the Ministry of the Center, as well as the {{ill|Shuyaku|ja|主鑰}} in the {{ill|Ōkura-shō|ja|大蔵省 (律令制)}} and the {{ill|Naizōryō|ja|内蔵寮}}. Originally, Kenmotsu may have been directly under the emperor as part of the "Shimotsumono-shoku" (下物職). This title was later incorporated into the Ministry of the Center under the Ritsuryō system. The term "Shimotsumono-shoku" persisted as an alternative name for Kenmotsu.
There were initially three grades of Kenmotsu: Dai Kenmotsu (大監物), Chū Kenmotsu (中監物), and Shō Kenmotsu (少監物). However, the Chū Kenmotsu position was later abolished and replaced by the newly established '''Kenmotsu Sakan''' (監物主典), who assisted with administrative duties.
== Ranks and Personnel == - '''Dai Kenmotsu''' (従五位下, Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade): 1 position - '''Chū Kenmotsu''' (従六位上, Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade): 4 positions (abolished) - '''Shō Kenmotsu''' (正七位下, Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade): 4 positions - '''Kenmotsu Sakan''' (従七位上, Junior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade): Newly established - '''Shisei''' (史生, Clerical officers)
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Government of Japan under the Ritsuryō system|ja|日本の官制}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Ritsuryō system of Japan]] [[Category:Government positions under the Ritsuryō system]]

{{Cite sources|date=March 2021}} '''Kenmotsu''' (監物) refers to a {{ill|Hinkan|ja|品官}} (specialist official) within the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, specifically under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Ministry of the Center|ja|中務省|1=Ministry of the Center}}.
== Duties == Kenmotsu officials were directly subordinate to the Ministry of the Center and were responsible for managing the keys and inspecting the inventory-related duties of various government offices' warehouses. They oversaw the actual handling of keys by officials such as the {{ill|Ten'yaku|ja|典鑰}} of the Ministry of the Center, as well as the {{ill|Shuyaku|ja|主鑰}} in the {{ill|Ōkura-shō|ja|大蔵省 (律令制)}} and the {{ill|Naizōryō|ja|内蔵寮}}. Originally, Kenmotsu may have been directly under the emperor as part of the "Shimotsumono-shoku" (下物職). This title was later incorporated into the Ministry of the Center under the Ritsuryō system. The term "Shimotsumono-shoku" persisted as an alternative name for Kenmotsu.
There were initially three grades of Kenmotsu: Dai Kenmotsu (大監物), Chū Kenmotsu (中監物), and Shō Kenmotsu (少監物). However, the Chū Kenmotsu position was later abolished and replaced by the newly established '''Kenmotsu Sakan''' (監物主典), who assisted with administrative duties.
== Ranks and Personnel == - '''Dai Kenmotsu''' (従五位下, Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade): 1 position - '''Chū Kenmotsu''' (従六位上, Junior Sixth Rank, Upper Grade): 4 positions (abolished) - '''Shō Kenmotsu''' (正七位下, Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade): 4 positions - '''Kenmotsu Sakan''' (従七位上, Junior Seventh Rank, Upper Grade): Newly established - '''Shisei''' (史生, Clerical officers)
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Government of Japan under the Ritsuryō system|ja|日本の官制}}

[[Category:qq]][[sn:Kenmotsu]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:監物]]

{{Cite sources|date=March 2021}} '''Kenmotsu''' (監物) refers to a {{ill|Hinkan|ja|品官}} (specialist official) within the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, specifically under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Ministry of the Center|ja|中務省|1=Ministry of the Center}}.
== Duties == Kenmotsu officials were directly subordinate to the Ministry of the Center and were responsible for managing the keys and inspecting the inventory-related duties of various government offices' warehouses. They oversaw the actual handling of keys by officials such as the {{ill|Ten'yaku|ja|典鑰}} of the Ministry of the Center, as well as the {{ill|Shuyaku|ja|主鑰}} in the {{ill|Ōkura-shō|ja|大蔵省 (律令制)}} and the {{ill|Naizōryō|ja|内蔵寮}}. Originally, Kenmotsu may have been directly under the emperor as part of the "Shimotsumono-shoku" (下物職). This title was later incorporated into the Ministry of the Center under the Ritsuryō system. The term "Shimotsumono-shoku" persisted as an alternative name for Kenmotsu.
There were initially three grades of Kenmotsu: Dai Kenmotsu (大監物), Chū Kenmotsu (中監物), and Shō Kenmotsu (少監物). However, the Chū Kenmotsu position was later abolished and replaced by the newly established '''Kenmotsu Sakan''' (監物主典), who assisted with administrative duties.
== Ranks and Personnel == - '''Dai Kenmotsu''' (従五位下, Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade): 1 position - '''Chū Kenmotsu''' (従六位上, Junior Sixth Rank, Uqqer Grade): 4 positions (abolished) - '''Shō Kenmotsu''' (正七位下, Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade): 4 positions - '''Kenmotsu Sakan''' (従七位上, Junior Seventh Rank, Uqqer Grade): Newly established - '''Shisei''' (史生, Clerical officers)
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Government of Japan under the Ritsuryō system|ja|日本の官制}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Ritsuryō system of Japan]] [[Category:Government positions under the Ritsuryō system]]

{{Cite sources|date=March 2021}} '''Kenmotsu''' (監物) refers to a {{ill|Hinkan|ja|品官}} (specialist official) within the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, specifically under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Ministry of the Center|ja|中務省|1=Ministry of the Center}}.
== Duties == Kenmotsu officials were directly subordinate to the Ministry of the Center and were responsible for managing the keys and inspecting the inventory-related duties of various government offices' warehouses. They oversaw the actual handling of keys by officials such as the {{ill|Ten'yaku|ja|典鑰}} of the Ministry of the Center, as well as the {{ill|Shuyaku|ja|主鑰}} in the {{ill|Ōkura-shō|ja|大蔵省 (律令制)}} and the {{ill|Naizōryō|ja|内蔵寮}}. Originally, Kenmotsu may have been directly under the emperor as part of the "Shimotsumono-shoku" (下物職). This title was later incorporated into the Ministry of the Center under the Ritsuryō system. The term "Shimotsumono-shoku" persisted as an alternative name for Kenmotsu.
There were initially three grades of Kenmotsu: Dai Kenmotsu (大監物), Chū Kenmotsu (中監物), and Shō Kenmotsu (少監物). However, the Chū Kenmotsu position was later abolished and replaced by the newly established '''Kenmotsu Sakan''' (監物主典), who assisted with administrative duties.
== Ranks and Personnel == - '''Dai Kenmotsu''' (従五位下, Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade): 1 position - '''Chū Kenmotsu''' (従六位上, Junior Sixth Rank, Uqqer Grade): 4 positions (abolished) - '''Shō Kenmotsu''' (正七位下, Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade): 4 positions - '''Kenmotsu Sakan''' (従七位上, Junior Seventh Rank, Uqqer Grade): Newly established - '''Shisei''' (史生, Clerical officers)
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Government of Japan under the Ritsuryō system|ja|日本の官制}}

revision.text:
{{Cite sources|date=March 2021}} '''Kenmotsu''' (監物) refers to a {{ill|Hinkan|ja|品官}} (specialist official) within the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, specifically under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Ministry of the Center|ja|中務省|1=Ministry of the Center}}.
== Duties == Kenmotsu officials were directly subordinate to the Ministry of the Center and were responsible for managing the keys and inspecting the inventory-related duties of various government offices' warehouses. They oversaw the actual handling of keys by officials such as the {{ill|Ten'yaku|ja|典鑰}} of the Ministry of the Center, as well as the {{ill|Shuyaku|ja|主鑰}} in the {{ill|Ōkura-shō|ja|大蔵省 (律令制)}} and the {{ill|Naizōryō|ja|内蔵寮}}. Originally, Kenmotsu may have been directly under the emperor as part of the "Shimotsumono-shoku" (下物職). This title was later incorporated into the Ministry of the Center under the Ritsuryō system. The term "Shimotsumono-shoku" persisted as an alternative name for Kenmotsu.
There were initially three grades of Kenmotsu: Dai Kenmotsu (大監物), Chū Kenmotsu (中監物), and Shō Kenmotsu (少監物). However, the Chū Kenmotsu position was later abolished and replaced by the newly established '''Kenmotsu Sakan''' (監物主典), who assisted with administrative duties.
== Ranks and Personnel == - '''Dai Kenmotsu''' (従五位下, Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade): 1 position - '''Chū Kenmotsu''' (従六位上, Junior Sixth Rank, Uqqer Grade): 4 positions (abolished) - '''Shō Kenmotsu''' (正七位下, Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade): 4 positions - '''Kenmotsu Sakan''' (従七位上, Junior Seventh Rank, Uqqer Grade): Newly established - '''Shisei''' (史生, Clerical officers)
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Government of Japan under the Ritsuryō system|ja|日本の官制}}

{{Cite sources|date=March 2021}} '''Kenmotsu''' (監物) refers to a {{ill|Hinkan|ja|品官}} (specialist official) within the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, specifically under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Ministry of the Center|ja|中務省|1=Ministry of the Center}}.

They oversaw the actual handling of keys by officials such as the {{ill|Ten'yaku|ja|典鑰}} of the Ministry of the Center, as well as the {{ill|Shuyaku|ja|主鑰}} in the {{ill|Ōkura-shō|ja|大蔵省 (律令制)}} and the {{ill|Naizōryō|ja|内蔵寮}}. Originally, Kenmotsu may have been directly under the emperor as part of the "Shimotsumono-shoku" (下物職). This title was later incorporated into the Ministry of the Center under the Ritsuryō system. The term "Shimotsumono-shoku" persisted as an alternative name for Kenmotsu.
There were initially three grades of Kenmotsu: Dai Kenmotsu (大監物), Chū Kenmotsu (中監物), and Shō Kenmotsu (少監物). However, the Chū Kenmotsu position was later abolished and replaced by the newly established '''Kenmotsu Sakan''' (監物主典), who assisted with administrative duties.
== Ranks and Personnel == - '''Dai Kenmotsu''' (従五位下, Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade): 1 position - '''Chū Kenmotsu''' (従六位上, Junior Sixth Rank, Uqqer Grade): 4 positions (abolished) - '''Shō Kenmotsu''' (正七位下, Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade): 4 positions - '''Kenmotsu Sakan''' (従七位上, Junior Seventh Rank, Uqqer Grade): Newly established - '''Shisei''' (史生, Clerical officers)

{{Cite sources|date=March 2021}} '''Kenmotsu''' (監物) refers to a {{ill|Hinkan|ja|品官}} (specialist official) within the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, specifically under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Ministry of the Center|ja|中務省|1=Ministry of the Center}}.
== Duties == Kenmotsu officials were directly subordinate to the Ministry of the Center and were responsible for managing the keys and inspecting the inventory-related duties of various government offices' warehouses. They oversaw the actual handling of keys by officials such as the {{ill|Ten'yaku|ja|典鑰}} of the Ministry of the Center, as well as the {{ill|Shuyaku|ja|主鑰}} in the {{ill|Ōkura-shō|ja|大蔵省 (律令制)}} and the {{ill|Naizōryō|ja|内蔵寮}}. Originally, Kenmotsu may have been directly under the emperor as part of the "Shimotsumono-shoku" (下物職). This title was later incorporated into the Ministry of the Center under the Ritsuryō system. The term "Shimotsumono-shoku" persisted as an alternative name for Kenmotsu.
There were initially three grades of Kenmotsu: Dai Kenmotsu (大監物), Chū Kenmotsu (中監物), and Shō Kenmotsu (少監物). However, the Chū Kenmotsu position was later abolished and replaced by the newly established '''Kenmotsu Sakan''' (監物主典), who assisted with administrative duties.
== Ranks and Personnel == - '''Dai Kenmotsu''' (従五位下, Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade): 1 position - '''Chū Kenmotsu''' (従六位上, Junior Sixth Rank, Uqqer Grade): 4 positions (abolished) - '''Shō Kenmotsu''' (正七位下, Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade): 4 positions - '''Kenmotsu Sakan''' (従七位上, Junior Seventh Rank, Uqqer Grade): Newly established - '''Shisei''' (史生, Clerical officers)

{{Cite sources|date=March 2021}} '''Kenmotsu''' (監物) refers to a {{ill|Hinkan|ja|品官}} (specialist official) within the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, specifically under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Ministry of the Center|ja|中務省|1=Ministry of the Center}}.
== Duties == Kenmotsu officials were directly subordinate to the Ministry of the Center and were responsible for managing the keys and inspecting the inventory-related duties of various government offices' warehouses. They oversaw the actual handling of keys by officials such as the {{ill|Ten'yaku|ja|典鑰}} of the Ministry of the Center, as well as the {{ill|Shuyaku|ja|主鑰}} in the {{ill|Ōkura-shō|ja|大蔵省 (律令制)}} and the {{ill|Naizōryō|ja|内蔵寮}}. Originally, Kenmotsu may have been directly under the emperor as part of the "Shimotsumono-shoku" (下物職). This title was later incorporated into the Ministry of the Center under the Ritsuryō system. The term "Shimotsumono-shoku" persisted as an alternative name for Kenmotsu.
There were initially three grades of Kenmotsu: Dai Kenmotsu (大監物), Chū Kenmotsu (中監物), and Shō Kenmotsu (少監物). However, the Chū Kenmotsu position was later abolished and replaced by the newly established '''Kenmotsu Sakan''' (監物主典), who assisted with administrative duties.
== Ranks and Personnel == - '''Dai Kenmotsu''' (従五位下, Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade): 1 position - '''Chū Kenmotsu''' (従六位上, Junior Sixth Rank, Uqqer Grade): 4 positions (abolished) - '''Shō Kenmotsu''' (正七位下, Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade): 4 positions - '''Kenmotsu Sakan''' (従七位上, Junior Seventh Rank, Uqqer Grade): Newly established - '''Shisei''' (史生, Clerical officers)

== Ranks and Personnel == - '''Dai Kenmotsu''' (従五位下, Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade): 1 position - '''Chū Kenmotsu''' (従六位上, Junior Sixth Rank, Uqqer Grade): 4 positions (abolished) - '''Shō Kenmotsu''' (正七位下, Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade): 4 positions - '''Kenmotsu Sakan''' (従七位上, Junior Seventh Rank, Uqqer Grade): Newly established - '''Shisei''' (史生, Clerical officers)

{{Cite sources|date=March 2021}} '''Kenmotsu''' (監物) refers to a {{ill|Hinkan|ja|品官}} (specialist official) within the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, specifically under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Ministry of the Center|ja|中務省|1=Ministry of the Center}}.
== Duties == Kenmotsu officials were directly subordinate to the Ministry of the Center and were responsible for managing the keys and inspecting the inventory-related duties of various government offices' warehouses. They oversaw the actual handling of keys by officials such as the {{ill|Ten'yaku|ja|典鑰}} of the Ministry of the Center, as well as the {{ill|Shuyaku|ja|主鑰}} in the {{ill|Ōkura-shō|ja|大蔵省 (律令制)}} and the {{ill|Naizōryō|ja|内蔵寮}}. Originally, Kenmotsu may have been directly under the emperor as part of the "Shimotsumono-shoku" (下物職). This title was later incorporated into the Ministry of the Center under the Ritsuryō system. The term "Shimotsumono-shoku" persisted as an alternative name for Kenmotsu.
There were initially three grades of Kenmotsu: Dai Kenmotsu (大監物), Chū Kenmotsu (中監物), and Shō Kenmotsu (少監物). However, the Chū Kenmotsu position was later abolished and replaced by the newly established '''Kenmotsu Sakan''' (監物主典), who assisted with administrative duties.
== Ranks and Personnel == - '''Dai Kenmotsu''' (従五位下, Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade): 1 position - '''Chū Kenmotsu''' (従六位上, Junior Sixth Rank, U11er Grade): 4 positions (abolished) - '''Shō Kenmotsu''' (正七位下, Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade): 4 positions - '''Kenmotsu Sakan''' (従七位上, Junior Seventh Rank, U11er Grade): Newly established - '''Shisei''' (史生, Clerical officers)

{{Cite sources|date=March 2021}} '''Kenmotsu''' (監物) refers to a {{ill|Hinkan|ja|品官}} (specialist official) within the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, specifically under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Ministry of the Center|ja|中務省|1=Ministry of the Center}}.
== Duties == Kenmotsu officials were directly subordinate to the Ministry of the Center and were responsible for managing the keys and inspecting the inventory-related duties of various government offices' warehouses. They oversaw the actual handling of keys by officials such as the {{ill|Ten'yaku|ja|典鑰}} of the Ministry of the Center, as well as the {{ill|Shuyaku|ja|主鑰}} in the {{ill|Ōkura-shō|ja|大蔵省 (律令制)}} and the {{ill|Naizōryō|ja|内蔵寮}}. Originally, Kenmotsu may have been directly under the emperor as part of the "Shimotsumono-shoku" (下物職). This title was later incorporated into the Ministry of the Center under the Ritsuryō system. The term "Shimotsumono-shoku" persisted as an alternative name for Kenmotsu.
There were initially three grades of Kenmotsu: Dai Kenmotsu (大監物), Chū Kenmotsu (中監物), and Shō Kenmotsu (少監物). However, the Chū Kenmotsu position was later abolished and replaced by the newly established '''Kenmotsu Sakan''' (監物主典), who assisted with administrative duties.
== Ranks and Personnel == * '''Dai Kenmotsu''' (従五位下, Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade): 1 position * '''Chū Kenmotsu''' (従六位上, Junior Sixth Rank, U11er Grade): 4 positions (abolished) * '''Shō Kenmotsu''' (正七位下, Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade): 4 positions * '''Kenmotsu Sakan''' (従七位上, Junior Seventh Rank, U11er Grade): Newly established * '''Shisei''' (史生, Clerical officers)
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Government of Japan under the Ritsuryō system|ja|日本の官制}}

{{Cite sources|date=March 2021}} '''Kenmotsu''' (監物) refers to a {{ill|Hinkan|ja|品官}} (specialist official) within the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, specifically under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Ministry of the Center|ja|中務省|1=Ministry of the Center}}.
== Duties == Kenmotsu officials were directly subordinate to the Ministry of the Center and were responsible for managing the keys and inspecting the inventory-related duties of various government offices' warehouses. They oversaw the actual handling of keys by officials such as the {{ill|Ten'yaku|ja|典鑰}} of the Ministry of the Center, as well as the {{ill|Shuyaku|ja|主鑰}} in the {{ill|Ōkura-shō|ja|大蔵省 (律令制)}} and the {{ill|Naizōryō|ja|内蔵寮}}. Originally, Kenmotsu may have been directly under the emperor as part of the "Shimotsumono-shoku" (下物職). This title was later incorporated into the Ministry of the Center under the Ritsuryō system. The term "Shimotsumono-shoku" persisted as an alternative name for Kenmotsu.
There were initially three grades of Kenmotsu: Dai Kenmotsu (大監物), Chū Kenmotsu (中監物), and Shō Kenmotsu (少監物). However, the Chū Kenmotsu position was later abolished and replaced by the newly established '''Kenmotsu Sakan''' (監物主典), who assisted with administrative duties.
== Ranks and Personnel == * '''Dai Kenmotsu''' (従五位下, Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade): 1 position * '''Chū Kenmotsu''' (従六位上, Junior Sixth Rank, U11er Grade): 4 positions (abolished) * '''Shō Kenmotsu''' (正七位下, Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade): 4 positions * '''Kenmotsu Sakan''' (従七位上, Junior Seventh Rank, U11er Grade): Newly established * '''Shisei''' (史生, Clerical officers)

revision.text:
{{Cite sources|date=March 2021}} '''Kenmotsu''' (監物) refers to a {{ill|Hinkan|ja|品官}} (specialist official) within the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, specifically under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Ministry of the Center|ja|中務省|1=Ministry of the Center}}.
== Duties == Kenmotsu officials were directly subordinate to the Ministry of the Center and were responsible for managing the keys and inspecting the inventory-related duties of various government offices' warehouses. They oversaw the actual handling of keys by officials such as the {{ill|Ten'yaku|ja|典鑰}} of the Ministry of the Center, as well as the {{ill|Shuyaku|ja|主鑰}} in the {{ill|Ōkura-shō|ja|大蔵省 (律令制)}} and the {{ill|Naizōryō|ja|内蔵寮}}. Originally, Kenmotsu may have been directly under the emperor as part of the "Shimotsumono-shoku" (下物職). This title was later incorporated into the Ministry of the Center under the Ritsuryō system. The term "Shimotsumono-shoku" persisted as an alternative name for Kenmotsu.
There were initially three grades of Kenmotsu: Dai Kenmotsu (大監物), Chū Kenmotsu (中監物), and Shō Kenmotsu (少監物). However, the Chū Kenmotsu position was later abolished and replaced by the newly established '''Kenmotsu Sakan''' (監物主典), who assisted with administrative duties.
== Ranks and Personnel == * '''Dai Kenmotsu''' (従五位下, Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade): 1 position * '''Chū Kenmotsu''' (従六位上, Junior Sixth Rank, U11er Grade): 4 positions (abolished) * '''Shō Kenmotsu''' (正七位下, Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade): 4 positions * '''Kenmotsu Sakan''' (従七位上, Junior Seventh Rank, U11er Grade): Newly established * '''Shisei''' (史生, Clerical officers)
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Government of Japan under the Ritsuryō system|ja|日本の官制}}

Originally, Kenmotsu may have been directly under the emperor as part of the "Shimotsumono-shoku" (下物職). This title was later incorporated into the Ministry of the Center under the Ritsuryō system. The term "Shimotsumono-shoku" persisted as an alternative name for Kenmotsu.

revision.comment: Text replacement - "大蔵省 (律令制)}}" to "大蔵省 (律令制)|1=Ministry of the Treasury}}"
revision.text:
{{Cite sources|date=March 2021}} '''Kenmotsu''' (監物) refers to a {{ill|Hinkan|ja|品官}} (specialist official) within the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, specifically under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Ministry of the Center|ja|中務省|1=Ministry of the Center}}.
== Duties == Kenmotsu officials were directly subordinate to the Ministry of the Center and were responsible for managing the keys and inspecting the inventory-related duties of various government offices' warehouses. They oversaw the actual handling of keys by officials such as the {{ill|Ten'yaku|ja|典鑰}} of the Ministry of the Center, as well as the {{ill|Shuyaku|ja|主鑰}} in the {{ill|Ōkura-shō|ja|大蔵省 (律令制)|1=Ministry of the Treasury}} and the {{ill|Naizōryō|ja|内蔵寮}}. Originally, Kenmotsu may have been directly under the emperor as part of the "Shimotsumono-shoku" (下物職). This title was later incorporated into the Ministry of the Center under the Ritsuryō system. The term "Shimotsumono-shoku" persisted as an alternative name for Kenmotsu.
There were initially three grades of Kenmotsu: Dai Kenmotsu (大監物), Chū Kenmotsu (中監物), and Shō Kenmotsu (少監物). However, the Chū Kenmotsu position was later abolished and replaced by the newly established '''Kenmotsu Sakan''' (監物主典), who assisted with administrative duties.
== Ranks and Personnel == * '''Dai Kenmotsu''' (従五位下, Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade): 1 position * '''Chū Kenmotsu''' (従六位上, Junior Sixth Rank, U11er Grade): 4 positions (abolished) * '''Shō Kenmotsu''' (正七位下, Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade): 4 positions * '''Kenmotsu Sakan''' (従七位上, Junior Seventh Rank, U11er Grade): Newly established * '''Shisei''' (史生, Clerical officers)
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Government of Japan under the Ritsuryō system|ja|日本の官制|1=Japanese administrative system}}

{{Cite sources|date=March 2021}} '''Kenmotsu''' (監物) refers to a {{ill|Hinkan|ja|品官}} (specialist official) within the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, specifically under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Ministry of the Center|ja|中務省|1=Ministry of the Center}}.
== Duties == Kenmotsu officials were directly subordinate to the Ministry of the Center and were responsible for managing the keys and inspecting the inventory-related duties of various government offices' warehouses. They oversaw the actual handling of keys by officials such as the {{ill|Ten'yaku|ja|典鑰}} of the Ministry of the Center, as well as the {{ill|Shuyaku|ja|主鑰}} in the {{ill|Ōkura-shō|ja|大蔵省 (律令制)|1=Ministry of the Treasury}} and the {{ill|Naizōryō|ja|内蔵寮}}. Originally, Kenmotsu may have been directly under the emperor as part of the "Shimotsumono-shoku" (下物職). This title was later incorporated into the Ministry of the Center under the Ritsuryō system. The term "Shimotsumono-shoku" persisted as an alternative name for Kenmotsu.
There were initially three grades of Kenmotsu: Dai Kenmotsu (大監物), Chū Kenmotsu (中監物), and Shō Kenmotsu (少監物). However, the Chū Kenmotsu position was later abolished and replaced by the newly established '''Kenmotsu Sakan''' (監物主典), who assisted with administrative duties.
== Ranks and Personnel == * '''Dai Kenmotsu''' (従五位下, Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade): 1 position * '''Chū Kenmotsu''' (従六位上, Junior Sixth Rank, U11er Grade): 4 positions (abolished) * '''Shō Kenmotsu''' (正七位下, Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade): 4 positions * '''Kenmotsu Sakan''' (従七位上, Junior Seventh Rank, U11er Grade): Newly established * '''Shisei''' (史生, Clerical officers)
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Government of Japan under the Ritsuryō system|ja|日本の官制|1=Japanese administrative system}}

== Ranks and Personnel == * '''Dai Kenmotsu''' (従五位下, Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade): 1 position * '''Chū Kenmotsu''' (従六位上, Junior Sixth Rank, U11er Grade): 4 positions (abolished) * '''Shō Kenmotsu''' (正七位下, Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade): 4 positions * '''Kenmotsu Sakan''' (従七位上, Junior Seventh Rank, U11er Grade): Newly established * '''Shisei''' (史生, Clerical officers)
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Government of Japan under the Ritsuryō system|ja|日本の官制|1=Japanese administrative system|12=draft}}

revision.text:
{{Cite sources|date=March 2021}} '''Kenmotsu''' (監物) refers to a {{ill|Hinkan|ja|品官|12=draft}} (specialist official) within the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制|12=draft}}, specifically under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Ministry of the Center|ja|中務省|1=Ministry of the Center|12=draft}}.
== Duties == Kenmotsu officials were directly subordinate to the Ministry of the Center and were responsible for managing the keys and inspecting the inventory-related duties of various government offices' warehouses. They oversaw the actual handling of keys by officials such as the {{ill|Ten'yaku|ja|典鑰|12=draft}} of the Ministry of the Center, as well as the {{ill|Shuyaku|ja|主鑰|12=draft}} in the {{ill|Ōkura-shō|ja|大蔵省 (律令制)|1=Ministry of the Treasury|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Naizōryō|ja|内蔵寮|12=draft}}. Originally, Kenmotsu may have been directly under the emperor as part of the "Shimotsumono-shoku" (下物職). This title was later incorporated into the Ministry of the Center under the Ritsuryō system. The term "Shimotsumono-shoku" persisted as an alternative name for Kenmotsu.
There were initially three grades of Kenmotsu: Dai Kenmotsu (大監物), Chū Kenmotsu (中監物), and Shō Kenmotsu (少監物). However, the Chū Kenmotsu position was later abolished and replaced by the newly established '''Kenmotsu Sakan''' (監物主典), who assisted with administrative duties.
== Ranks and Personnel == * '''Dai Kenmotsu''' (従五位下, Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade): 1 position * '''Chū Kenmotsu''' (従六位上, Junior Sixth Rank, U11er Grade): 4 positions (abolished) * '''Shō Kenmotsu''' (正七位下, Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade): 4 positions * '''Kenmotsu Sakan''' (従七位上, Junior Seventh Rank, U11er Grade): Newly established * '''Shisei''' (史生, Clerical officers)
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Government of Japan under the Ritsuryō system|ja|日本の官制|1=Japanese administrative system|12=draft}}

revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2021年3月}} '''監物'''（けんもつ）は、[[律令制]]において[[中務省]]に属した[[官人#品官|品官]]（ほんかん）。
== 職掌 == 監物は中務省直属の官で諸官庁の倉庫の鍵を管理・出納事務の監察に携わった。そのため鍵を実際に取り扱う中務省の[[典鑰]]や[[大蔵省 (律令制)|大蔵省]]・[[内蔵寮]]の主鑰を実質的に統率した。もともとは[[天皇]]直属の「下物職」であったらしく律令施行によって中務省に組み込まれた。下物職の名称は監物の別名として使われることがある。大・中・少の三員あったが中監物は廃止され替わりに'''監物主典'''（けんもつさかん）が設置されて事務を補助した。
== 職員 == *大監物（[[従五位|従五位下]]）1名 *中監物（[[従六位|従六位上]]）4名・廃止 *少監物（[[正七位|正七位下]]）4名 *監物主典（[[従七位|従七位上]]） 新設 *[[史生]]
== 関連項目 == *[[日本の官制]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:けんもつ}} [[category:律令制の官制]]

They oversaw the actual handling of keys by officials such as the {{ill|Ten'yaku|ja|典鑰|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten'yaku|1=Minor Key-Holder|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11393761}} of the Ministry of the Center, as well as the {{ill|Shuyaku|ja|主鑰|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuyaku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the [[:en:Ministry of the Treasury|Ministry of the Treasury]] and the {{ill|Naizōryō|ja|内蔵寮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naizōryō|1=kuraryou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11394423}}. Originally, Kenmotsu may have been directly under the emperor as part of the "Shimotsumono-shoku" (下物職). This title was later incorporated into the Ministry of the Center under the Ritsuryō system. The term "Shimotsumono-shoku" persisted as an alternative name for Kenmotsu.
There were initially three grades of Kenmotsu: Dai Kenmotsu (大監物), Chū Kenmotsu (中監物), and Shō Kenmotsu (少監物). However, the Chū Kenmotsu position was later abolished and replaced by the newly established '''Kenmotsu Sakan''' (監物主典), who assisted with administrative duties.
== Ranks and Personnel == * '''Dai Kenmotsu''' (従五位下, Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade): 1 position * '''Chū Kenmotsu''' (従六位上, Junior Sixth Rank, U11er Grade): 4 positions (abolished) * '''Shō Kenmotsu''' (正七位下, Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade): 4 positions * '''Kenmotsu Sakan''' (従七位上, Junior Seventh Rank, U11er Grade): Newly established * '''Shisei''' (史生, Clerical officers)
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Government of Japan under the Ritsuryō system|ja|日本の官制|1=structure of the ritsuryō government|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Government of Japan under the Ritsuryō system|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese administrative system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8193883}}

== Duties == Kenmotsu officials were directly subordinate to the Ministry of the Center and were responsible for managing the keys and inspecting the inventory-related duties of various government offices' warehouses. They oversaw the actual handling of keys by officials such as the {{ill|Ten'yaku|ja|典鑰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten'yaku|1=Minor Key-Holder|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11393761}} of the Ministry of the Center, as well as the {{ill|Shuyaku|ja|主鑰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuyaku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the [[:en:Ministry of the Treasury|Ministry of the Treasury]] and the {{ill|Naizōryō|ja|内蔵寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naizōryō|1=kuraryou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11394423}}. Originally, Kenmotsu may have been directly under the emperor as part of the "Shimotsumono-shoku" (下物職). This title was later incorporated into the Ministry of the Center under the Ritsuryō system. The term "Shimotsumono-shoku" persisted as an alternative name for Kenmotsu.
There were initially three grades of Kenmotsu: Dai Kenmotsu (大監物), Chū Kenmotsu (中監物), and Shō Kenmotsu (少監物). However, the Chū Kenmotsu position was later abolished and replaced by the newly established '''Kenmotsu Sakan''' (監物主典), who assisted with administrative duties.
== Ranks and Personnel == * '''Dai Kenmotsu''' (従五位下, Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade): 1 position * '''Chū Kenmotsu''' (従六位上, Junior Sixth Rank, U11er Grade): 4 positions (abolished) * '''Shō Kenmotsu''' (正七位下, Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade): 4 positions * '''Kenmotsu Sakan''' (従七位上, Junior Seventh Rank, U11er Grade): Newly established * '''Shisei''' (史生, Clerical officers)
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Government of Japan under the Ritsuryō system|ja|日本の官制|1=structure of the ritsuryō government|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Government of Japan under the Ritsuryō system|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese administrative system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8193883}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|監物|version=87952719|comment=Imported full ja history}}

revision.comment: v25: Convert interwiki links [[:en:...]] to {{ill}} templates
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:監物]][[zh:監物]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:監物]][[zh:監物]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:監物]][[zh:監物]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11581472]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11581472]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=March 2021}} '''Kenmotsu''' (監物) refers to a {{ill|Hinkan|ja|品官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinkan|qq=}} (specialist official) within the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system}}, specifically under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Ministry of the Center|en|Ministry of the Center|WD=Q6867690|lt=Ministry of the Center}}.
== Duties == Kenmotsu officials were directly subordinate to the Ministry of the Center and were responsible for managing the keys and inspecting the inventory-related duties of various government offices' warehouses. They oversaw the actual handling of keys by officials such as the {{ill|Ten'yaku|ja|典鑰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten'yaku|1=Minor Key-Holder|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11393761}} of the Ministry of the Center, as well as the {{ill|Shuyaku|ja|主鑰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuyaku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the {{ill|Ministry of the Treasury|en|Ministry of the Treasury|WD=Q112323848|lt=Ministry of the Treasury}} and the {{ill|Naizōryō|ja|内蔵寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naizōryō|1=kuraryou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11394423}}. Originally, Kenmotsu may have been directly under the emperor as part of the "Shimotsumono-shoku" (下物職). This title was later incorporated into the Ministry of the Center under the Ritsuryō system. The term "Shimotsumono-shoku" persisted as an alternative name for Kenmotsu.
There were initially three grades of Kenmotsu: Dai Kenmotsu (大監物), Chū Kenmotsu (中監物), and Shō Kenmotsu (少監物). However, the Chū Kenmotsu position was later abolished and replaced by the newly established '''Kenmotsu Sakan''' (監物主典), who assisted with administrative duties.
== Ranks and Personnel == * '''Dai Kenmotsu''' (従五位下, Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade): 1 position * '''Chū Kenmotsu''' (従六位上, Junior Sixth Rank, U11er Grade): 4 positions (abolished) * '''Shō Kenmotsu''' (正七位下, Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade): 4 positions * '''Kenmotsu Sakan''' (従七位上, Junior Seventh Rank, U11er Grade): Newly established * '''Shisei''' (史生, Clerical officers)
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Government of Japan under the Ritsuryō system|ja|日本の官制|1=structure of the ritsuryō government|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Government of Japan under the Ritsuryō system|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese administrative system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8193883}}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|監物|version=87952719|comment=Imported full ja history}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:監物]][[zh:監物]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:監物]][[zh:監物]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:監物]][[zh:監物]]
{{Unreferenced|date=March 2021}} '''Kenmotsu''' (監物) refers to a {{ill|Hinkan|ja|品官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinkan|qq=}} (specialist official) within the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system|1=Ritsuryō}}, specifically under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Ministry of the Center|en|Ministry of the Center|WD=Q6867690|lt=Ministry of the Center|1=Ministry of the Center}}.
== Duties == Kenmotsu officials were directly subordinate to the Ministry of the Center and were responsible for managing the keys and inspecting the inventory-related duties of various government offices' warehouses. They oversaw the actual handling of keys by officials such as the {{ill|Ten'yaku|ja|典鑰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten'yaku|1=Minor Key-Holder|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11393761}} of the Ministry of the Center, as well as the {{ill|Shuyaku|ja|主鑰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shuyaku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the {{ill|Ministry of the Treasury|en|Ministry of the Treasury|WD=Q112323848|lt=Ministry of the Treasury|1=Ministry of the Treasury}} and the {{ill|Naizōryō|ja|内蔵寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naizōryō|1=kuraryou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11394423}}. Originally, Kenmotsu may have been directly under the emperor as part of the "Shimotsumono-shoku" (下物職). This title was later incorporated into the Ministry of the Center under the Ritsuryō system. The term "Shimotsumono-shoku" persisted as an alternative name for Kenmotsu.
There were initially three grades of Kenmotsu: Dai Kenmotsu (大監物), Chū Kenmotsu (中監物), and Shō Kenmotsu (少監物). However, the Chū Kenmotsu position was later abolished and replaced by the newly established '''Kenmotsu Sakan''' (監物主典), who assisted with administrative duties.
== Ranks and Personnel == * '''Dai Kenmotsu''' (従五位下, Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade): 1 position * '''Chū Kenmotsu''' (従六位上, Junior Sixth Rank, U11er Grade): 4 positions (abolished) * '''Shō Kenmotsu''' (正七位下, Senior Seventh Rank, Lower Grade): 4 positions * '''Kenmotsu Sakan''' (従七位上, Junior Seventh Rank, U11er Grade): Newly established * '''Shisei''' (史生, Clerical officers)
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Government of Japan under the Ritsuryō system|ja|日本の官制|1=structure of the ritsuryō government|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Government of Japan under the Ritsuryō system|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese administrative system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8193883}}
